scenarios.title: Redispatch partially completed mowing after equipment failure
scenarios.trigger: A hydraulic-pressure alarm stops undervine mowing after 11.2 of 18.6 acres are complete.
scenarios.goal: Retain trustworthy partial progress, block the remaining assignment while equipment is unavailable, redispatch it after repair, and verify full-block actuals without erasing history.
scenarios.context: Carneros Ridge Block 07 has a mapped weed escape before an irrigation set. The field team records partial scope at the row level while the manager and operations coordinator supervise several simultaneous crews from desktop dispatch.
preconditions: The observation and work order share BLK-007
The work is approved before the coordinator assigns it
The foreman acknowledges current instructions before execution
missing_information: Exact repair completion time is unknown when the partial result and blocker are first recorded
normal_path: Viticulturist scopes the recommended mowing work
Coordinator assigns the work and the foreman acknowledges it
Foreman starts execution and reports actual acreage
exception_path: Foreman records 11.2 safely completed acres when the hydraulic alarm stops execution
Coordinator creates a remaining-scope assignment for rows 28–34
Manager blocks that assignment while the mower remains unavailable
recovery_path: Maintenance replaces the leaking hose and pressure-tests the mower
Coordinator redispatches only the remaining rows
Foreman acknowledges the current assignment before resuming execution
Manager verifies the full acreage and immutable exception history
branches: If repair evidence is absent, remain blocked
If the remaining scope changes, supersede or reassign it without rewriting the partial event
If actual acreage exceeds block acreage, require correction before verification
communications: Foreman reports partial scope and the equipment alarm to management
Coordinator redispatches the repaired remaining-scope assignment to the foreman and field crew
scenarios.device_context: Field report on mobile; cross-crew redispatch and verification on responsive desktop
scenarios.connectivity: A field update may be captured offline; synchronization preserves it as an immutable note when the server has already advanced.
compliance_implications: Actual equipment and labor time remains distinguishable from planned time
Original partial and blocked events remain attributable after verified closure
scenarios.completion_condition: WO-001 is verified at 18.6 completed acres, its remaining-scope assignment followed canonical redispatch and acknowledgement states, and its partial and blocked history remains inspectable.

expected_transitions.reason: Viticulturist records an attributable field observation
Viticulturist scopes method, timing, and constraints
Manager approves the routine work plan
Coordinator dispatches accountable assignees and evidence requirements
Foreman confirms current instructions and constraints
Foreman preserves safe acreage after the hydraulic alarm
Coordinator redispatches the remaining scope after repair
Foreman acknowledges the repaired remaining-scope assignment
Foreman reports final actual scope and acreage
